title: UCI Regression Project Datasets
text:
1 Overview
This Quarto project is configured as a book.
It currently includes:

uci_regression_datasets.qmd for the UCI regression dataset collection
datasets.qmd for the earlier mixed dataset notes

Render the project with:
quarto render

title: 2  Notes
text:
3 Clean datasets
This document lists UCI datasets that are potentially useful for regression projects.
For each dataset below, I include:
Most import examples use the {ucimlrepo} package, which can fetch datasets directly from the UCI Machine Learning Repository from within R.

title: 2  Notes
text:
3.1 Student Performance
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/320/student+performance

student_performance <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 320)
student_performance_data <- student_performance$data$original

dim(student_performance_data)

[1] 649  33

head(student_performance_data)

5        0 11 13 13

Number of features: 30
Number of targets: 3
Number of observations: 649
Response variable: official targets are G1, G2, and G3; for regression, G3 is the usual choice
Topic: student achievement in secondary education

title: 2  Notes

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.1 Adult
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/2/adult

adult <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 2)
adult_data <- adult$data$original

dim(adult_data)

[1] 48842    15

head(adult_data)

  age        workclass fnlwgt education education-num     marital-status
1  39        State-gov  77516 Bachelors            13      Never-married
2  50 Self-emp-not-inc  83311 Bachelors            13 Married-civ-spouse
3  38          Private 215646   HS-grad             9           Divorced
4  53          Private 234721      11th             7 Married-civ-spouse
5  28          Private 338409 Bachelors            13 Married-civ-spouse
6  37          Private 284582   Masters            14 Married-civ-spouse
         occupation  relationship  race    sex capital-gain capital-loss
1      Adm-clerical Not-in-family White   Male         2174            0
2   Exec-managerial       Husband White   Male            0            0
3 Handlers-cleaners Not-in-family White   Male            0            0
4 Handlers-cleaners       Husband Black   Male            0            0
5    Prof-specialty          Wife Black Female            0            0
6   Exec-managerial          Wife White Female            0            0
  hours-per-week native-country income
1             40  United-States  <=50K
2             13  United-States  <=50K
3             40  United-States  <=50K
4             40  United-States  <=50K
5             40           Cuba  <=50K
6             40  United-States  <=50K



Number of features: 14
Number of targets: 1
Number of observations: 48,842
Response variable: if you want regression, a workable quantitative outcome is hours-per-week
Topic: census-style demographic and employment data
Missing-data handling: convert ? placeholders to NA
Parsing / encoding: one-hot encode categorical predictors such as work class, education, marital status, occupation, relationship, race, sex, and native country
Other preparation: remove or redefine the original classification target if it is not part of the regression task

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.2 Air Quality
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/360/air+quality

air_quality <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 360)
air_quality_data <- air_quality$data$original

dim(air_quality_data)

[1] 9357   15

head(air_quality_data)

       Date     Time CO(GT) PT08.S1(CO) NMHC(GT) C6H6(GT) PT08.S2(NMHC) NOx(GT)
1 3/10/2004 18:00:00    2.6        1360      150     11.9          1046     166
2 3/10/2004 19:00:00    2.0        1292      112      9.4           955     103
3 3/10/2004 20:00:00    2.2        1402       88      9.0           939     131
4 3/10/2004 21:00:00    2.2        1376       80      9.2           948     172
5 3/10/2004 22:00:00    1.6        1272       51      6.5           836     131
6 3/10/2004 23:00:00    1.2        1197       38      4.7           750      89
  PT08.S3(NOx) NO2(GT) PT08.S4(NO2) PT08.S5(O3)    T   RH     AH
1         1056     113         1692        1268 13.6 48.9 0.7578
2         1174      92         1559         972 13.3 47.7 0.7255
3         1140     114         1555        1074 11.9 54.0 0.7502
4         1092     122         1584        1203 11.0 60.0 0.7867
5         1205     116         1490        1110 11.2 59.6 0.7888
6         1337      96         1393         949 11.2 59.2 0.7848



Number of features: 14
Number of targets: 1
Number of observations: 9,357
Response variable: a reasonable regression target is one ground-truth pollutant measurement, such as CO(GT)
Topic: air pollution measurements from gas sensors
Missing-data handling: convert the original missing-value code, commonly -200, to NA
Parsing / encoding: parse and combine the date and time fields into a usable timestamp
Other preparation: after choosing one (GT) pollutant as the target, drop the other (GT) pollutant columns and keep the sensor, weather, and time-based variables as predictors

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.3 Individual Household Electric Power Consumption
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/235/individual+household+electric+power+consumption

household_power <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 235)
household_power_data <- household_power$data$original

dim(household_power_data)
head(household_power_data)


Number of features: 9
Number of targets: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of observations: 2,075,259
Response variable: a natural regression target is Global_active_power
Topic: household electricity consumption over time
Missing-data handling: convert blank measurement fields to NA; the UCI page notes about 1.25% missing rows in the measurements
Parsing / encoding: parse Date and Time, combine them into a timestamp, and convert imported text columns to numeric values
Merging / reshaping: none

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.4 Beijing Multi-Site Air Quality
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/501/beijing+multi+site+air+quality+data

beijing_air_quality <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 501)
beijing_air_quality_data <- beijing_air_quality$data$original

dim(beijing_air_quality_data)
head(beijing_air_quality_data)


Number of features: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of targets: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of observations: 420,768
Response variable: a natural regression target is one pollutant such as PM2.5, PM10, or O3
Topic: multi-site air-pollution and weather measurements in Beijing
Missing-data handling: keep, drop, or impute rows with missing values; the UCI page states that missing data are denoted as NA
Parsing / encoding: parse year, month, day, and hour into a timestamp
Merging / reshaping: unzip and stack the multiple site files into one table, keeping station as a grouping variable if needed

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.5 SML2010
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/274/sml2010

sml2010 <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 274)
sml2010_data <- sml2010$data$original

dim(sml2010_data)
head(sml2010_data)


Number of features: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of targets: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of observations: 4,137
Response variable: a natural regression target is an indoor temperature variable such as the dining-room or room temperature
Topic: smart-home environmental sensor measurements
Missing-data handling: inspect and handle possible missing values; the UCI page says the dataset could contain missing values
Parsing / encoding: parse the UTC Date and Time fields and skip the commented header lines that begin with #
Merging / reshaping: combine NEW-DATA-1.T15.txt and NEW-DATA-2.T15.txt into one dataset

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.6 Online Retail II
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/502/online+retail+ii

online_retail_ii <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 502)
online_retail_ii_data <- online_retail_ii$data$original

dim(online_retail_ii_data)
head(online_retail_ii_data)


Number of features: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of targets: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of observations: 1,067,371
Response variable: a workable quantitative target is UnitPrice or an aggregated revenue outcome
Topic: multi-year online retail transactions
Preprocessing needed: handle missing values, especially in customer-level fields
Preprocessing needed: parse InvoiceDate as a date-time variable
Preprocessing needed: split or merge the Excel workbook sheets into one consistent transaction table before modeling

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.6 Open University Learning Analytics Dataset
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/349/open+university+learning+analytics+dataset

oulad <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 349)
oulad_data <- oulad$data$original

dim(oulad_data)
head(oulad_data)


Number of features: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of targets: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of observations: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Response variable: a workable quantitative target is assessment score after joining the student and assessment tables
Topic: student activity, registration, and assessment behavior in online courses
Missing-data handling: inspect and handle missing values; the UCI page marks the dataset as having missing values
Parsing / encoding: convert date-like offset fields such as registration day, assessment day, and VLE interaction day into a consistent course-time scale
Merging / reshaping: join the linked tables such as studentAssessment.csv, studentInfo.csv, studentRegistration.csv, studentVle.csv, and vle.csv

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.3 Restaurant & consumer data
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/232/restaurant+consumer+data

restaurant_consumer <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 232)
restaurant_consumer_data <- restaurant_consumer$data$original

dim(restaurant_consumer_data)
head(restaurant_consumer_data)


Number of features: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of targets: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of observations: the main rating table has 1,161 user-place ratings; the auxiliary tables range from 130 to 2,339 rows
Response variable: a clear regression target is rating from rating_final.csv
Topic: restaurant recommendation with user, place, and context attributes
Missing-data handling: inspect and handle missing values in files such as geoplaces2.csv and userprofile.csv
Parsing / encoding: encode the many categorical preference and restaurant-description variables
Merging / reshaping: join rating_final.csv with the user and restaurant tables using userID and placeID

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.4 Communities and Crime Unnormalized
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/211/communities+and+crime+unnormalized

communities_crime <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 211)
communities_crime_data <- communities_crime$data$original

dim(communities_crime_data)
head(communities_crime_data)


Number of features: 125
Number of targets: 18 possible crime outcomes are described on the UCI page
Number of observations: 2,215
Response variable: a clear regression target is ViolentCrimesPerPop
Topic: socio-economic, law-enforcement, and crime measurements for U.S. communities
Missing-data handling: inspect and handle the many missing values, especially in the law-enforcement variables
Parsing / encoding: encode categorical identifiers such as State if they are retained
Merging / reshaping: none in the distributed file, but the UCI page notes that the source data were originally combined from several datasets
Other preparation: choose one crime variable as the target and remove overlapping crime subtotal variables that would leak information into that target

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.5 KDD Cup 1998
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/129/kdd+cup+1998

kdd_cup_1998 <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 129)
kdd_cup_1998_data <- kdd_cup_1998$data$original

dim(kdd_cup_1998_data)
head(kdd_cup_1998_data)


Number of features: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of targets: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of observations: 191,779
Response variable: a clear regression target is TARGET_D, the donation amount in the original KDD target files
Topic: direct-marketing donation modeling
Missing-data handling: inspect and handle the missing values noted on the UCI page
Parsing / encoding: encode the large number of categorical marketing and demographic variables
Merging / reshaping: join the learning file with the provided target file information when reconstructing a modeling table

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.6 Real Estate Valuation
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/477/real+estate+valuation+data+set

real_estate <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 477)
real_estate_data <- real_estate$data$original

dim(real_estate_data)
head(real_estate_data)


Number of features: 6
Number of targets: 1
Number of observations: 414
Response variable: Y house price of unit area
Topic: housing valuation in New Taipei City
Missing-data handling: none indicated on the UCI page
Parsing / encoding: convert X1 transaction date from its decimal-year format into a more interpretable time representation if needed
Merging / reshaping: import the Excel file cleanly and standardize variable names if desired

title: 2  Notes
text:
4.7 UJIIndoorLoc
Webpage: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/310/ujiindoorloc

ujiindoorloc <- fetch_ucirepo(id = 310)
ujiindoorloc_data <- ujiindoorloc$data$original

dim(ujiindoorloc_data)
head(ujiindoorloc_data)


Number of features: not clearly specified on the UCI page
Number of targets: 4 position-related outputs are described on the UCI page
Number of observations: 21,048 total, from 19,937 training rows and 1,111 validation rows
Response variable: for regression, the clearest targets are Longitude and Latitude
Topic: indoor localization from WiFi fingerprints
Missing-data handling: none indicated on the UCI page, but the value 100 should be treated as a special code for undetected WAPs rather than as an ordinary numeric signal
Parsing / encoding: parse the UNIX Timestamp if time effects matter and decide how to treat categorical fields such as BuildingID, SpaceID, UserID, and PhoneID
Merging / reshaping: combine trainingData.csv and validationData.csv if you want one unified analysis table
